response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web engineer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and D language background, but they lack nearly all core requirements for this role: no native-level Japanese proficiency, no demonstrated Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, no networking/security domain work (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching), and only brief C/C++ exposure (a ~7-month stint at National Instruments). Their embedded experience is minimal and unrelated to LAN-switch firmware. This is a fundamental mismatch with the position's hard requirements.
strengths: Long, varied software development career (15+ years) with strong testing and craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD)
Some systems-level exposure: C/C++ components at National Instruments, low-latency D services, Linux experience
Proven ownership and delivery across diverse stacks, including open-source project leadership
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — a hard requirement for the role
No experience with networking protocols, switch products, security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X), or Linux kernel modification
C proficiency is dated and brief; career trajectory is firmly web/full-stack, not embedded firmware

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or Linux kernel experience
Broad engineering experience and quality practices
